name: Logo Split / Lockup Pulse
one_line: A two-part mark sits joined at center, splits left↔right to open a gap, grows a center word-lockup one word per onset (key word lands on the downbeat surge), then snap-closes on a hit and pulses with the beat.

match.notes: A short logo/brand sting, not a typed sentence. Discriminated from typewriter-phrase-keyword-shuffle by density (dense, not sparse/medium-only) + has_roll:true (a sustained-fill hihat bed it consumes as a PULSE, not a cascade) + the left/right bracketing-mark roles. Reveals are still per-onset (split -> word -> word), so the structural_type is shared, but the build-face is the logo lockup, not text typing. The KEY content word should be placed on the strongest hit (downbeat / SURGE). If the grid goes quiet (onset_gap_bars >= ~1.5) this is the wrong template — use a hold template.

examples.brand: Lille — World Design Capital 2020 (left radial spark + 'lille 2020' + right cloud)

left_mark.notes: Left BRACKET of the logo (group_7: a radial spark; mass on the OUTER/left side, opening toward the text — see one_line). Appears joined at center on the entry onset (f2), then GLIDES left to the FULL lockup width on the split onset (f6) — fast launch + expo.out settle, smoothed by velocity motion blur. Holds open; does NOT track each word. PROPOSED verb 'split_open' (paired symmetric translate; reverse = 'snap_close').

right_mark.notes: Right BRACKET (group_7: a cloud / backwards-3 swirl; mass OUTER/right, opening toward the text). Mirrors left_mark exactly — same beats, same offset (the two always correspond / move together). Pre-opens to the full width so even word1 alone floats in wide-open brackets (faithful: the marks barely move when more words fill in).

lockup.notes: 1..N words ACCUMULATING one per onset into the reserved center space (not replace). Each word eases in with a soft stretch (scaleX 0.58->1, ~0.24s power3.out — smooth, NO poppy overshoot). The KEY word sits on the downbeat/SURGE (group_7: 'lille'@0.25 then '2020'@1.0 on SURGE@1s).

close.notes: On a key hit (group_7: f50 / 2.083s) the lockup snap-closes: words HARD-CUT to gone (0ms, no squash) AND both brackets teleport back together in the SAME frame — the teleport reads as an impact streak through the motion blur. PROPOSED verb 'snap_close'.

seesaw.notes: After the snap the welded brackets do a DAMPED VERTICAL SPRING + a rigid SEE-SAW TILT (rotation +deg = left-spark UP / right-cloud DOWN), beat-synced to the hi-hat hits after the snap (group_7: f51 slam-down lowest, f55 up-rebound, f58 dip, f63 flick). Amplitude decays; the unit settles slightly HIGH and TILTED (spark up/cloud down) into the f70 cut. ~95% vertical translate + a building rotation. NOT a scale-pulse, NOT per-side independent — it's one rigid unit rotating. PROPOSED verb 'seesaw_bounce'.

background.notes: Flat solid color, dead still the whole span (group_7: green #249b5f). No color change. Optional energy_envelope_breath add-on, not required.

leftMark.notes: inline SVG (or one half of a single logo). Empty = the default radial spark. Keep the mass on the OUTER side so it reads as a bracket.

rightMark.notes: inline SVG (or the other half). Empty = the default cloud/3 swirl. Mass on the OUTER side.

beat_map.comment: Script-side musical timing constants in index.html, filled from <group>.audiomap.json. Content lives in params; musical timing lives here. All times are ABSOLUTE audio seconds. The marks move on these DISCRETE anchors only — NOT on the sustained hi-hat fill (texture).
beat_map.word_onsets_notes: one per non-empty word, in order; word_onsets[0] doubles as the split. Add more entries for 3+ words. The strongest hit (here 1.0 = SURGE@1s downbeat) should carry the key word.
beat_map.seesaw_note: f51/f55/f58/f63 — welded unit damped vertical bounce + see-saw tilt (left up/right down), decaying, settling high+tilted to the cut. These ARE beat-synced (the one place after the snap that tracks the hi-hats).
beat_map.exit_note: logo holds/drifts to 2.875 (f69) then the source hard-cuts to the next group at f70 (2.917, near f71 kick)

uses_primitives_notes: split_open / snap_close / settle_drift are proposed custom phrases the impl defines (paired symmetric translate, 0ms collapse, slow drift). radial_burst_lines (#25) backs the default left spark; kinetic_letter_in (#02) backs the word stretch-pop. SILKINESS comes from the registry 'motion-blur' snippet (velocity-driven SVG ghost trail) attached to the marks + expo/power eases over longer durations — NOT from extra keyframes.
bundled_assets: assets/gsap.min.js (offline GSAP), assets/fredoka-700.woff2 (heavy rounded face for the words — declared @font-face 'Lockup'). The motion-blur snippet is inlined in index.html (no external file).
